mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 9.1% signals weakening labor market conditions that reduce corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or UNRATE declines below 8.5% without corresponding equity gains.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP and housing starts indicates strong aggregate demand, which drives corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-GDP/high-housing scenarios are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production below 94.04 indicates reduced manufacturing activity, which lowers corporate revenues and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or INDPRO rises above 95 without corresponding equity declines.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles, indicated by rising federal funds rates, increase borrowing costs and reduce equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of high-FEDFUNDS periods, or Rank IC > 0.05 in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 131,132 thousand indicates robust employment, which boosts consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or PAYEMS declines below 130,000 without corresponding equity losses.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in residential construction permits above 604 thousand signals heightened demand for housing, which stimulates economic activity and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or HOUST declines below 600 without corresponding equity declines.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising inflation and tightening monetary policy creates uncertainty, which depresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC > 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in high-CPI/high-FEDFUNDS scenarios are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
